notes: bible arc 1 (onboarding-stall), before-checkpoint: kickoff cadence still weekly, champion responsive, day-0 case open but unremarkable; trend signals correctly return insufficient-history.
bible arc 1, during-checkpoint: two dispatch-integration cases fired, reply latency stretching, Rocketlane phase overdue.
bible arc 1, after-checkpoint: post-recovery, cadence back to weekly, steady_state/green already fired.

bible arc 2 (single-threaded-risk): single point of failure exposed at day 10 (champion quiet since day 3); health band doesn't move until day 14, so the recommendation must come from communication signals, not the band.
bible arc 2: second contact surfaced (Monica Reeves, day 110) but not yet champion of record -- still a gap until day 110's NewContactAppears is followed up on.
bible arc 2: recovered, thread width restored, StakeholderRelationship strength strong for Monica Reeves -- scripted CSM (Priya Nandan) already engaged.
bible arc 3 (churn-brewing): red since day 0, no replacement contact ever surfaces; health band gives no new signal at any checkpoint.
bible arc 3: day-160 renewal case unanswered by this checkpoint -- absence despite an already-flagged risk.
bible arc 3: post-churn (StatusChange to Churned at day 220) -- the gap was never remediated.
bible arc 4 (silent-decline): decline just starting; zero cases, zero CTAs, health band stays green -- only a UsageSignal trend read surfaces the risk.
bible arc 4: usage down ~7% cumulative, band still green, zero cases/CTAs -- nothing in the CRM/CS-platform view looks wrong.
bible arc 4: decline continued to ~12% cumulative, still under the 20% auto-adjustment threshold, still green.
bible arc 5 (expansion-ready): new department stakeholder (Sarah Chen) just onboarded day 10, usage climbing.
bible arc 5: usage sustained high, ARR expansion about to close at day 180 -- ideal agent flags expansion-readiness pre-confirmation.
bible arc 5: expansion closed (ARRChange day 180), year-end uptick in progress.
bible arc 6 (healthy-control): no risk, no expansion trigger, no onboarding gap -- the baseline every other arc's briefing is judged against.
bible arc 6: off-beat from the mild summer dip, chosen specifically to read as unambiguously fine.
bible arc 6: year-end uptick in progress, still boringly fine.
bible red herring A: usage telemetry alone reads as an early risk signal, but the CRM case + day-30 renewal-closed beat say this was renewal paperwork friction the whole time.
bible red herring B: the case subject/priority alone pattern-matches Pinehill's onboarding-stall signature, but the fast resolution + untouched usage/calendar say it was a same-week glitch.
tier-mirror 1 (champion-quiet), high-touch: ChampionGoesQuiet day 130; personal_email allowed at this tier.
tier-mirror 1 (champion-quiet), tech-touch: same ChampionGoesQuiet day 130 as ironclad-freight, but personal_email is forbidden at tech_touch -- the anti-pattern test for this mirror.
tier-mirror 2 (shallow-adoption), mid-touch: route_optimization entitled-but-shallow; correct motion is content_route citing playbooks.json's reactivate-stalled-module catalog ids.

tier-mirror 2 (shallow-adoption), high-touch: same signal as brookstone-supply-chain, but the correct motion is a live working_session, not a routed content asset -- the tier-differentiation this mirror tests.

tier-mirror 3 (cohort truth): one of 25 tech-touch accounts sharing the identical Route Optimizer shallow-adoption pattern by day 140 -- eval/tier_policy_battery.py asserts the whole cohort together yields exactly ONE cohort_action, never 25 individual per-account motions.

Harvest 6 (Report 24): the tier-gating proof account -- tech_touch, Driver Coaching entitled but underused (deliberately not Route Optimizer, to stay out of tier-mirror 3's 25-account cohort and campaigns.py's TARGET_COHORT), one consenting contact, quiet/no-arc. Its own resolved motion is content_route, never personal_email its tier forbids -- this is what makes eval/tier_gating_battery.py's real-sweep-guard-fleetops case non-vacuous (accounts_checked >= 1, no vacuous_pass key).
